page: Amazon Web Services r8gd.xlarge is a memory-optimized instance powered by AWS Graviton processors. It features 4 physical cores and 4 vCPUs on the arm64 architecture, utilizing the AWS Neoverse-V2 CPU model running at 2.8 GHz. The instance is equipped with 32.0 GB of system memory, yielding an 8.0 GB memory-to-core ratio. Local storage is provided via a 237 GB NVMe SSD instance store volume, and network connectivity is supported by a 2 Gbps baseline bandwidth.

Benchmark results place the r8gd.xlarge in the average performance tier for most standard operations. It achieves a single-core stress-ng score of 1707.39 and a multi-core score of 6817.45. Memory bandwidth is measured at 462.09 GB/sec for cached small blocks and 96.75 GB/sec for uncached large blocks. The instance demonstrates strong single-threaded bzip3 decompression performance at 14.27 GB/sec, placing it in the top 25% of tested hardware. However, cryptographic performance is a notable tradeoff, with the PassMark CPU encryption test scoring 751.42, which falls into the bottom 10%. Large-model LLM text generation is also limited, yielding 6.18 tokens/sec on a 7B model.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the r8gd.xlarge offers high memory density and integrated local NVMe storage on an energy-efficient ARM architecture, reducing the need for external block storage for temporary data. This hardware profile is well-suited for memory-intensive workloads that benefit from local scratch space, such as caching layers, database operations, and web serving.
description: Amazon Web Services r8gd.xlarge is an arm64 memory-optimized instance featuring 4 dedicated physical cores powered by AWS Graviton Neoverse-V2 processors running at 2.8 GHz. It is configured with 32.0 GB of RAM, providing a high 8.0 GB per core ratio, alongside a 237 GB local NVMe SSD instance store and 2 Gbps baseline network bandwidth. Performance benchmarks indicate average capabilities across standard compute and memory tasks, highlighted by a strong single-threaded bzip3 decompression rate of 14.27 GB/sec, though cryptographic operations present a performance tradeoff. The combination of high memory density, local NVMe storage, and ARM-based architecture makes this instance a cost-efficient choice for database operations, caching layers, and memory-intensive web serving workloads.
og_description: Amazon Web Services r8gd.xlarge is an arm64 memory-optimized instance with 4 Graviton vCPUs, 32 GB RAM, and a 237 GB NVMe SSD, delivering balanced memory and local storage performance for databases.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services r8gd.xlarge is an arm64 memory-optimized instance featuring 4 vCPUs, 32 GB RAM, and 237 GB of local NVMe SSD storage.
tagline: An ARM-powered, memory-optimized instance featuring dedicated cores and local NVMe SSD storage for memory-intensive database and caching workloads.
bullet_points: 4 dedicated physical ARMv9 cores running at 2.8 GHz
32.0 GB of system memory with 8.0 GB per core
237 GB of high-speed local NVMe SSD storage
Tradeoffs in cryptographic and large-model LLM inference speeds
Suited for databases, caching layers, and web serving
